title: Interdiction Outcomes
query: SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Interdicted WHERE Submitted = 0 AND event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate) AS escaped, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Interdicted WHERE Submitted = 1 AND event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate) AS submitted, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM EscapeInterdiction WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate) AS count

details.text: {count} interdiction(s) evaded before the mass-lock completed, {escaped} fought off after refusing submission, and {submitted} conceded to during the report period. Interdictions are forced frame shift disengagements used by hostile ships to pull commanders out of supercruise into normal space.
details.help: count = EscapeInterdiction events (evaded before being pulled); escaped = Interdicted WHERE Submitted=false (fought the pull and won); submitted = Interdicted WHERE Submitted=true. count drives the title as the positive survival metric.

tagLines: Interdiction event log reviewed. Evasion and submission ratios noted in your flight operational record.
Some you dodged, some you fought, some you just let happen. This is that story.
The interdiction pull is the galaxy's way of asking: how badly do you want to get away?
